title: Module 1
content:
Crystallography:- Crystal, space lattice, unit cell- BCC,FCC, HCP structures,
Coordination number and radius ratio, Polymorphism and allotropy. Phase
diagrams: - solid solutions, Hume Rothery`s rule - equilibrium diagram of
common types of binary systems: Cu-Ni, Pb-Sn. Detailed discussion on Iron-
Carbon equilibrium diagram with microstructure and properties. Heat
treatments: - annealing, normalizing, hardening, spheroidizing. Tempering.
Surface hardening methods: - no change in surface composition methods: -
Flame, induction, laser and electron beam hardening processes- change in
surface composition methods: carburizing and Nitriding; applications.

title: Module 2
content:
Applications of ferrous and non ferrous alloys steel - low, medium, high
carbon steels, stainless steels, tool steels . Cast iron-  gray, white, ductile cast
irons. copper and its alloys, aluminium and its alloys, magnesium and alloys,
Composites:- –classification - fiber phase: - characteristics, classifications -
matrix phase:- functions, characteristics of PMC, MMC, and CMC,
applications of composites .ceramics, polymers- thermo plastics and
thermosetting,  Nano materials, bio materials, Optical fibers.

title: Module 3
content:
Primary processes – Casting – sand casting, moulds, types of moulding
sand,types of patterns, pattern materials – Forging – forging methods,
precision forging, coining, heading, piercing, die design, die materials and
lubrication, forging machines – Forming – Extrusion process, hot extrusion,
cold

title: Module 4
content:
Extrusion, impact extrusion, wire drawing process – Rolling – principles of
rolling, types of rolling mill, hot and cold rolling, rolling mills, ring rolling,
thread rolling, applications – rolling of tubes, wheels, axles and I-beams.
Importance of material selection for manufacturing processes. Selection of
primary forming process and suitable materials for various products. Defects
and Design considerations in casting, forging and rolling.
